task_completion.description: Verify that the 5 specified bookmark folders have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Bookmarks Bar or the Bookmark Manager (chrome://bookmarks). You should see folders named 'International Organizations', 'European Union', 'Latin America', 'Research Databases', and 'Data & Visualization'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the specified languages are added and spell check is enabled for them.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Chrome Languages settings page (chrome://settings/languages). Spanish (es), French (fr), and Portuguese - Brazil (pt-BR) should be listed under preferred languages, and the spell check toggles for these languages should be turned on.

task_completion.description: Verify that the specified Chrome experimental flags are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Chrome Flags page (chrome://flags). The flags 'Smooth Scrolling' (smooth-scrolling), 'Parallel downloading' (enable-parallel-downloading), and 'Tab Scrolling' (tab-scrolling) should be visible and their dropdown menus should be set to 'Enabled' (often highlighted in blue).

task_completion.description: Verify that the custom search engine shortcuts are set up.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Manage search engines and site search page (chrome://settings/searchEngines). Under 'Site search' or custom search engines, there should be entries with the specific keywords/shortcuts: 'oecd', 'scholar', and 'wb'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the default download directory has been changed to the specified folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Chrome Downloads settings page (chrome://settings/downloads). The 'Location' path should end with 'Research_Data'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Chrome graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Chrome settings pages, bookmark manager, and flags pages being navigated and interacted with.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing Chrome configuration files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'nano', 'vim', 'jq', or 'sqlite3' being used to directly modify files in the ~/.config/google-chrome/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the application being configured is the genuine Google Chrome browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI should match Google Chrome, including the omnibox (address bar), standard Chrome tabs, and Chrome-specific internal URLs (chrome://).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Bookmark Folders: 'International Organizations', 'European Union', 'Latin America', 'Research Databases', 'Data & Visualization'. Expected Languages: Spanish (es), French (fr), Portuguese-Brazil (pt-BR). Expected Chrome Flags to enable: 'smooth-scrolling', 'enable-parallel-downloading', 'tab-scrolling'. Expected Search Engine Shortcuts/Keywords: 'oecd', 'scholar', 'wb'. Expected Download Directory: 'Research_Data'.
